title: Bridge Essay: Psilocybin for treatment-resistant depression: fMRI-measured brain mechanisms.
concept: Psychedelic decoupling — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states of consciousness.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nonordinary — psychedelic decoupling, ego dissolution, DMN disruption, altered states.
**Phase context:** Psychedelic decoupling — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states of consciousness.

**Psilocybin for treatment-resistant depression: fMRI-measured brain mechanisms.** — Sci Rep (2017). DOI: 10.1038/s41598-017-13282-7. PMID: 29030624. Psilocybin with psychological support is showing promise as a treatment model in psychiatry but its therapeutic mechanisms are poorly understood. Here, cerebral blood flow (CBF) and blood oxygen-level dependent (BOLD) resting-state functional connectivity (RSFC) were measured with functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) before and after treatment with psilocybin (serotonin agonist) for treatment-resistant depression (TRD). Quality pre and post treatment fMRI data were collected from 16 of 1
